Okay, welcome back. In this lesson, we are going to discuss some of the more common challenges you are going to run into as you coach your pre customers, your pre clients through their situational gestalts. So of course, there is a whole range of issues, challenges, difficulties you might run into. But in my experience, they will typically all fall into one of these three categories, defending, distancing or dissociating and deflecting. So this is really what I've discovered to be the 95 five of what you may encounter. And in this lesson, I really want to expose and explore what these three are, how to recognize them. And then most importantly, how to get your enrollment conversations back on track and leaning towards an empowered decision by the end of it. So let's explore all three of them. separately, all of them, however, are essentially responses to not feeling safe or powerful. And this is why we put so much emphasis throughout this program on those three metastates, because ultimately, the resolution of these three challenges are resolved through reestablishing or strengthening those three metastates. So these are things we have to establish early and reinforce often. So what do they actually look like? How do you recognize them? Well, defending is when they take your questions personally, and all of a sudden shift into defensiveness to protect themselves from feeling vulnerable, or protect themselves from feeling their own guilt and shame around a particular situation. So this might appear in response to a very matter of fact question, right, where you're just mirroring back what you're hearing and trying to get clarity for your own understanding, right, factual information. So you might ask, so what I'm hearing is that you haven't sold any of your art pieces since last December, right, very simple question. And rather than a very clear yes or no response, you might get this whole spin that starts off. Well, no, that's not really the case. I was supposed to everything was lined up and you've got and the gallery was going to purchase all 30 pieces and it was going to make my whole income for the year and everything was going to be awesome. And then my agent fucked it up, right, and then they might go on to spin this whole rant around what that looks like, right, essentially playing some form of victim being in a state of blame, and just defending themselves from their own guilt and shame around how they are responsible for the shortcomings of their situation. So what do you do when this arises? Well, you can allow them to briefly indulge in it, right, ever so briefly, just indulge in it, let it unwind, let it process, let them feel an experience of blame and victimhood at least for a moment, right, so you can prompt them with a question. All right, you know, tell me a little bit more about that, tell me about how that played out. And they may just continue to discharge, right, they might feel a form of catharsis. So don't sit there agreeing with, Oh, that sucks, I really see what you mean, you really got fucked over that time. So you're not here to validate the falsehoods, but you're here to allow some of that to unwind, so that they have spacious spaciousness to explore new possibilities within it, right, so when the spin feels complete, this is your opportunity to re invite them into a state of power and resourcefulness with a follow up question. So that might look like so knowing that agents can't be depended on, what would you like to do about it now, right, so whatever they're blaming, right, it might be their business partner, it might be their spouse, it might be the former gym they used to go to, it might be anything that they have gone into this blame victimhood defensive spin around why they haven't gone the result. And then you could ask after that spin is somewhat complete, you know, knowing that this is true, right, what would you like to do about it now and this should shift and reorient them back into that state of resourcefulness. Next thing you might want to do, right, is if they are still in that blame is get a little deeper to the root of the problem and you do this once again through an invitation through asking not stating not dictating not preaching, but simply asking is it possible that your anger and upsetness about this situation isn't so much about the agent, but about how dependent and how out of control you feel, right about how your livelihood is literally dependent on someone and something you have no control over is it possible that this is really the real issue here. So you're not stating it, you're offering that information and they can agree they could disagree they can have their own insight and epiphany and say, Oh my goodness, right, that's exactly it. I really hate not having control over my life and my career, right, and this might be the core root issue within that that they wish to solve. So once again, that epiphany that they may have in getting to the root of the problem should invite them to a state of clarity, resourcefulness and empowerment, where they can finally do something about it, right, the problem has now accurately been named. Next problem you might run into is distancing and dissociation. So this is when your pre customer may protect themselves with short answers, right, so they're not opening up fully, they're not being fully honest and transparent. And they're just willfully keeping things on the surface level, even when you have your deep questioning, even with your prompts to go deeper and explore and unpack the gestalt more fully, they're just kind of short in their answers and trying to keep things surfacing. So they are in fact distancing themselves from their own situation, right, and it's going to feel like that they're going to describe things almost as if they're observing their reality, they're the narrator of this movie that they're not implicated in, rather than one that they've actually experienced, right, so they're not connected to the full emotional experience of the situation that kind of pulled themselves out of it. And they're just kind of telling this bland story that they refuse to implicate themselves within, right, so it's void of emotion. And there are two reasons this will typically happen. First one, they could be guarding against you. And this isn't a personal thing, this could just be their response to those, you know, selling programs, enrolling in programs, it could be a response to past negative experiences with salespeople, where they feel like anything they say, share or do can and will be held against them at the point of close, right, so this might be why they're keeping things surface level, non emotional, because they don't want you to weaponize that against them later. So this will be clear, this will be evident when you sense that there is a bit of a coldness to their answer, a bit of a sharpness, a bit of like, who the fuck are you to ask that, right, I'm not going to give you all this information, I don't trust you to handle this information in a way that is truly to my benefit. So you're going to sense that if there is a bit of coldness. Next thing they might be guarding against is against their own shame, right, this is where they describe everything with kind of this casual blasé matter of factness, while still being friendly, right, so this is the typical telling the story without actually implicating themselves within the story, they don't want to be the character they are narrating because there's just too much shame in being it, right, they don't want to view themselves as the person who has struggled in that category, they don't want to view themselves as the person who has a relationship on the brink, they don't want to view themselves as the person who's been in business for two years, and has barely gotten traction, right, so they'll tell that story with that detached frame, right, so they're guarding against their own shame in that case, whichever one it is, it is your duty, it is your responsibility to reopen them, right, because the conversation just isn't going to yield a result, they're not going to have the catharsis of actually experiencing a breakthrough, unless they open up and re implicate themselves in their own lives. So this is where regardless of whether it is one or number two, you can ask them to reopen, you could ask them to self-check, can I be honest, I'm sensing that you're a bit guarded around this topic, and our work requires a high level of openness to really get into the details of your situation, so with no wrong answer, I just want to ask, are you sure you're ready to explore this topic with me? So in doing that, you'll both invite them into greater courage, and have them exercising power in making a conscious choice, right, and re giving their consent to continue, and at that same time, they get to abide by your boundaries, right, you don't want to just be doing this dance for 55 minutes straight, if they're not going to engage with you, you have every right as a coach, as an expert, as a professional, to guard your own energy, guard your own space, right, and if this person, and their defensiveness, and their detachment, and their association isn't allowing for that conversation to be fruitful, you have every right to self-check and make sure that they're ready to engage with you on this level, because if they're distancing, if they're defending at this stage, chances are, you're not going to be able to get a breakthrough with them on the other side of defense, right, when they're when they're in your actual program, so it's a great self-check to see if they are ready to step into greater transparency, trust, and openness with you. Cool, last one, deflecting, so this is a very common one, I believe most of us listening to this, watching this, have experienced this multiple times over, but this is where they'll essentially try to get on the same level as you, and this will come into form of probably asking some personal questions back to you, to reciprocate the question you asked of them, so if you're asking them about their life, about their business, they might turn it back, have you ever felt that in your own relationship business, they might ask you how you overcame your own problems or issues, they might ask you what that looks like for you, so all these things may come to the surface, and this is stuff that is indicative of them just not feeling fully comfortable going deeper, so on the surface, it may appear friendly, but in most cases, it's a deflection of not wanting to go deeper, or to resolve their own discomfort with a perceived power or safety imbalance. Remember, in module one, we spoke about this inherent imbalance of power on these calls, where you're the expert and you're asking them to be open, vulnerable, all these things, so they may try to reconcile that imbalance unconsciously by flipping questions back on you, by offering feedback to your situations, by commending you and congratulating you on how you've overcome certain things, and all this is not done maliciously, it's not done to consciously deflect the situation, it's just kind of a natural mechanism that happens when people feel a little less than safe and powerful in that relationship, so here's what to do, if it happens once, you can give a brief answer and get it back on track, but if you do see a pattern beginning to form, it's super important to intervene, you can have the call get derailed with you being asked to give your experience or give your feedback or open up to reciprocate every answer they give, so this is where you can intervene with a very clear, a very friendly, I appreciate you asking and I'm happy to go there another time, but I really do want to honor our intention for this call today, and keep it focused on giving you clarity around X, does that feel good? All right, so these are the typical issues, challenges you're going to run into, I encourage you to study this multiple times and get into habit of identifying which challenge is appearing on your calls, and getting into habit once again of intervening with questions, with statements that get your prospect back into a place of safety and power, so that's it for now, take a breather and I'll meet you in the next lesson.
